graphics-texture-filtering.reasoning:
可用來源與重點：
- [1] 官方 1.21.11 更新說明：Texture Filtering 為 Video Settings 新增選項；用途是控制貼圖在「斜角或遠距離」時的取樣方法；模式為 None/RGSS/Anisotropic；預設取決於 graphics preset 與硬體；RGSS 的定義（shader-based、提高清晰度、moderate performance cost、所有硬體可用）；Anisotropic 的定義（hardware-based、最佳清晰度、2x/4x/8x 由獨立滑桿控制、不支援則無效、會顯著影響 video memory usage，且高 mipmap count 時更明顯）。
- [4] Pre-Release 1 官方說明，內容與 [1] 同項可交叉印證。

內容結構規劃：
1) 用「遠看斜放的印刷圖案」類比建立直覺（為何斜角/距離會需要不同取樣）。
2) 給出 Texture Filtering 的官方定義與它在設定中的位置。[1]
3) 逐一說明三種模式中，官方有明確描述的 RGSS 與 Anisotropic 的運作性質與代價；對 None 僅說明它是可選值、官方未在該段落補充細節以避免過度推論。[1]
4) 強調實務決策點：畫質 vs（RGSS 的）效能成本，以及 Anisotropic 對顯示記憶體占用的顯著影響、尤其在高 mipmap count 時。[1]
graphics-texture-filtering.explanation:
想像你把一本有細密格紋的雜誌斜斜放在桌上：近看正面時很清楚，但只要你站遠一點、或從很斜的角度看，格紋就更容易變糊或出現不穩定的細節。Minecraft 1.21.11 的「Texture Filtering」就是在處理這類情境：它是 Video Settings 裡新增的選項，用來控制貼圖在「從距離觀看或以斜角觀看」時，遊戲要用什麼方式去取樣（sample）貼圖，讓畫面呈現更符合你的需求。[1]

在這個選項中，官方列出三種可選值：None、RGSS、Anisotropic，而實際預設會依你的 graphics preset 與硬體而定。[1] 其中 RGSS（Rotated Grid Super Sampling）被官方定義為「以 shader 實作的過濾方法」，能提升貼圖清晰度，但會帶來「中等」的效能成本，而且所有硬體都可使用。[1] Anisotropic 則是「以硬體實作的過濾方法」，提供最佳貼圖清晰度；其最大等級可用獨立滑桿調整為 2x、4x、8x，但若硬體不支援，開啟後也不會有作用。[1]

實務上，你最需要記住的取捨是「顯示記憶體」：官方特別提醒 Anisotropic Filtering 會顯著影響 video memory usage，並且在你同時使用較高的 mipmap count 時尤其明顯。[1] 因此調整 Texture Filtering 不只是“看起來更清楚”，也涉及你願意為畫面清晰度付出多少資源成本。
key_points: Texture Filtering 是 1.21.11 在 Video Settings 新增的圖形選項，用來控制貼圖在斜角或遠距離觀看時的取樣方法。[1]
可選模式包含 None、RGSS、Anisotropic，而預設值會依 graphics preset 與硬體而定。[1]
RGSS 是 shader-based 的過濾方法，可提升貼圖清晰度，但有中等效能成本，且所有硬體都可使用。[1]
Anisotropic 是 hardware-based 的過濾方法，提供最佳清晰度；最大等級可由獨立滑桿設為 2x/4x/8x，且硬體不支援時將不會生效。[1]
官方指出 Anisotropic Filtering 會顯著影響顯示記憶體占用，尤其在高 mipmap count 的情況下更明顯。[1]
examples: 情境：你常在山頂或飛行時遠看大片地表，想改善遠距離／斜角看到的貼圖清晰度；此時可嘗試在 Texture Filtering 於 RGSS 或 Anisotropic 間調整，比較清晰度差異（選項用途與模式見官方說明）。[1]
情境：你把 Anisotropic 等級拉到 8x，同時又使用較高的 mipmap count；官方提醒這種組合會讓 video memory usage 的影響更顯著，因此你應把它視為「記憶體占用」的取捨，而不只是畫面觀感調整。[1]
情境：你切到 Anisotropic 後視覺上沒有任何改變；官方指出若硬體不支援 Anisotropic Filtering，它將不會有作用，因此需先理解這是硬體支援條件所致。[1]

sources.title: Minecraft Java Edition 1.21.11
sources.snippet:
### Texture Filtering

*   New option in Video Settings
*   Controls the method used to sample textures when they are viewed at an angle or from a distance
*   Available values are "None", "RGSS", and "Anisotropic"
*   The default depends on your graphics preset and hardware
*   "RGSS" (Rotated Grid Super Sampling) is a shader-based filtering method that improves texture clarity at a moderate performance cost
    *   This is available on all hardware

*   "Anisotropic" is a hardware-based filtering method that provides the best texture clarity
    *   The maximum anisotropy may be "2x", "4x", and "8x", controllable by a separate slider
    *   If your hardware does not support it, Anisotropic Filtering will do nothing
    *   This significantly impacts video memory usage (especially combined with a high mipmap count)

Changes
-------

*   Horses, Mules, Donkeys, and Camels no longer sink in water while ridden by a player
    *   Skeleton Horses are an exception - they still sink

*   Bats no longer spawn more frequently and in higher light levels between October 20 and November 3
*   Items will no longer "bob" whenever their durability changes, such as when losing durability from an attack, or repaired by Mending
*   Horses, Mules, Donkeys, Camels, Zombie Horses and Camel Husks will not panic if controlled by Mobs
*   Parrots can now mimic the sounds of Zombie Nautilus, Zombie Horse, Camel Husk and Parched
*   Spawn Eggs have been reorganized into groups in the Creative Inventory
*   Chunks now fade in instead of appearing out of nowhere, with the fade-in time adjustable through Video Settings
*   Changes to visuals and visual options
*   Updated the Main Menu background panorama
*   Added splash text "One does not simply walk to the Far Lands"

> **Developer’s Note:**_This statement was proven false on October 4, 2025._

### Changes to Visuals

*   Graphics modes "Fast", "Fancy", and "Fabulous!" have been replaced with graphics presets with the same names
*   Cloud movement is now the same for all players on a server
*   All blocks in the world now have mipmaps applied if enabled in video settings
    *   Blocks that did not have mipmaps before, such as different vines, rails, foliage (apart from leaves) and more should look better from higher distances

*   Client FPS is expected to be higher for most players
*   Underwater biome fog color and fog distance is now blended based on the player's position between biomes, similarly to regular biome fog and sky colors
*   Environmental Fog in the Nether is no longer dependent on the render distance setting, and will consistently be applied from 10 to 96 blocks
*   The color of the sky during sunrises and sunsets is now affected by rain and thunder
*   When the "See-Through Leaves" video setting is disabled, the gaps between leaves are now dark green instead of black
*   Turning the "See-Through Leaves" video setting off now gives a bigger performance boost than before

#### Graphics Presets

*   Settings on the Video Settings screen have been reordered and split into 3 sections: "Display", "Quality & Performance", and "Preferences"
*   The following settings that were duplicating accessibility settings have been removed from the Video Settings screen:
    *   "View Bobbing"
    *   "Glint Speed"
    *   "Glint Strength"
    *   "FOV Effects"
    *   "Distortion Effects"

*   Tweaks that were previously hidden behind the "Graphics" setting have been split out to be configurable independently:
    *   "See-Through Leaves"
    *   "Show Vignette"
    *   "Weather Effect Radius" configures the distance in blocks within which rain and snow are visible
    *   "Improved Transparency" resolves some of the transparent geometry rendering issues at a significant GPU cost, previously was hidden behind "Fabulous!"

*   A new "Preset" slider was added to the top of the "Quality & Performance" section
    *   Presets, as you would expect based on gaming industry standard, control most of the "Quality & Performance" settings at once, not only the ones that were previously hidden behind the "Graphics" setting
    *   Available presets are "Fast", "Fancy", "Fabulous!" and "Custom"
    *   The default preset is "Fancy" for new players
    *   But for players who are upgrading to this version the preset will be set to "Custom" to preserve your current configuration

survival-loop.reasoning: 可用來源與重點： [1] 第一夜生存：白天先收集木頭/圓石/食物；日落前蓋「完整封閉」避難所；火把=木棍+煤/木炭；木炭可用熔爐燒木；熔爐=8圓石；夜晚待室內；飢餓過低無法回復生命；不要直挖下去。 [2] 生存基礎：生存模式需維持生命與飢餓；火把由煤+木棍；可用種子種小麥；熔爐可冶煉與煮食；避難所需門與火把；留意飢餓條。 [3] 十點建議：快速避難所（挖山壁或小木屋）；用火把照明避免黑暗；收集食物；避開敵對生物；床可設定重生點並跳過夜晚。 內容結構規劃：用「露營入夜前」類比→定義『生存基本循環』→三支柱（食物/照明/隔離）說明其因果（飢餓影響回血、黑暗生成敵對生物、封閉結構降低遭遇戰）→落到實務價值（穩定度過第一夜、為隔天資源擴張做準備）並用來源對應。
survival-loop.explanation:
把第一天想成「傍晚前要搭好營地」：你不需要豪華基地，但必須在天黑前把吃的、照明與遮蔽一次到位，才不會在夜裡被迫硬撐。

所謂「生存基本循環」，就是在白天優先蒐集食物與基礎資源、做出照明，並在日落前完成一個可封閉的簡易避難所；夜晚則盡量待在室內，等天亮再繼續擴張與升級。[1][3]

它之所以有效，靠的是三個因果鏈：第一，飢餓值太低時無法自然回復生命，因此要主動取得（並可用熔爐烹飪）食物，避免在戰鬥或意外後「回不回來」。[1][2] 第二，黑暗會讓敵對生物出現，因此用火把把住處照亮能降低風險；火把可由木棍與煤（或木炭）製作，而木炭可透過熔爐燒木取得。[1][2][3] 第三，避難所只要「完整封閉」，用門或方塊封口，就能把夜晚的危險轉成可控的門口管理問題。[1][2]

掌握這個循環，你就能把第一夜的重點從『打怪求生』改成『先確保生存條件』，隔天更有餘裕去挖礦、升級工具與規劃下一步。[1][3]
key_points: 白天優先把「食物＋照明＋避難所」做齊，夜晚則以待在室內、避免不必要交戰為原則。[1][3]
飢餓過低會影響生命回復，因此要確保穩定食物來源，並可用熔爐烹飪以提高可用性。[1][2]
黑暗會帶來敵對生物風險，火把照明是早期最直接的降風險手段；火把由木棍與煤/木炭製作，木炭可用熔爐燒木取得。[1][2][3]
簡易避難所的標準不是好看，而是「完整封閉」並用門或方塊封住入口，先把夜晚危險隔離在外。[1][2]
避免直挖下去以降低掉入熔岩或深洞等早期致命意外的機率。[1][2]
examples: 情境 1（找不到煤）：你先用圓石合成熔爐（需要 8 個圓石），把木頭拿去燒成木炭，再用木炭＋木棍做火把，立刻補上住處照明。[1][2]
情境 2（日落將近）：你直接挖進山壁做一個小空間，入口用方塊封口或放門，再在室內插火把；天黑後待在裡面等到天亮，降低遭遇敵對生物的風險。[1][2][3]
情境 3（夜裡受傷）：你發現自己飢餓值偏低時不易回復生命，因此優先吃掉手上的食物，讓後續的回血更有保障，避免被下一次攻擊帶走。[1][2]

sources.title: The Best Ways to Survive Your First Night in Minecraft
sources.snippet:
## 1. Gather Essential Resources Quickly

Time is limited when you start a new world, so act fast! Here’s what to collect in the first few minutes:

### What to Gather:

* **Wood**: Break trees to collect at least 10 logs. Wood is the most basic resource needed for crafting tools and a shelter.
* **Crafting Table**: Craft a crafting table from 4 wooden planks. This is essential for creating more advanced tools.
* **Wooden Pickaxe**: Use your crafting table to create a wooden pickaxe. This will allow you to mine stone and gather more useful materials.
* **Stone**: Mine at least 20 cobblestones to upgrade to better tools, like a stone pickaxe and stone sword.
* **Food**: Find and kill animals such as pigs, cows, or chickens to collect food.

## 2. Build a Shelter Before Nightfall

As the sun starts setting, you need a safe place to hide from the hostile mobs that spawn in the dark. Here are a few options for building a shelter:

### Shelter Ideas:

* **Simple Hut**: Build a small, simple house with dirt, wood, or stone. Ensure it’s fully enclosed, including a door or a block to block mobs from entering.
* **Underground Shelter**: If you’re near a hill, you can dig into the side of it to create a temporary shelter. Dig a small space, cover the entrance with dirt or wood, and stay inside until morning.
* **Village Shelter**: If you’re lucky enough to find a village, take shelter in one of the existing houses. These will usually already have a door and are safe from mobs.

Remember, your shelter doesn’t need to be fancy, just safe. The goal is to stay protected from mobs during the night.

## 3. Craft Torches to Light Up Your Shelter

Once inside your shelter, you’ll need to light it up to prevent hostile mobs from spawning. Darkness inside your shelter can still cause mobs to spawn, so make sure to create light sources.

### How to Make Torches:

* **Torches**: Combine sticks and coal (or charcoal) to craft torches. Coal can be mined from coal ore blocks found in caves, or you can craft charcoal by smelting wood in a furnace. Place torches around your shelter to keep it well-lit.
* **Campfires**: You can craft a campfire using sticks, wood, and coal or charcoal. It not only lights up your shelter but also allows you to cook food.

Lighting up your shelter will keep hostile mobs away and make your first night safer.

## 4. Craft Basic Survival Tools

With your shelter and torches in place, it’s time to craft some basic tools. These tools will help you survive the night and prepare for the next day.

### Essential Tools to Craft:

* **Iron Sword**: Crafted from iron and sticks, this sword will help you defend against hostile mobs like zombies and skeletons.
* **Iron Pickaxe**: Upgrade your stone pickaxe to a iron one. This will allow you to mine more valuable resources such as diamonds and gold.
* **Furnace**: A furnace is essential for cooking food and smelting ores. Craft a furnace using 8 cobblestones, and use it to cook meat or smelt ores into ingots.

[How To Find Diamonds Fast In Minecraft? The Ultimate Guide!

Learn how to quickly find diamonds in Minecraft with our ultimate guide! Discover the best mining techniques and levels to collect your Diamonds!

PlanetNode BlogFrederick Huizinga

](https://blog.planetnode.net/how-to-find-diamonds-fast-in-minecraft-the-ultimate-guide/)

## 5. Stay Safe and Avoid Common Dangers

While you wait out the night in your shelter, it’s important to avoid common mistakes that could put you in danger.

### Top Survival Tips:

* **Stay Indoors**: Mobs spawn in dark areas, so stay inside your shelter to avoid being attacked by zombies, skeletons, or creepers.
* **Don’t Dig Straight Down**: This is one of Minecraft’s most common pieces of advice. Digging straight down can cause you to fall into lava or a deep cave, putting your life at risk.
* **Monitor Your Hunger**: If your hunger bar gets too low, you won’t be able to regenerate health. Keep an eye on your food and eat whenever your hunger bar is low to stay healthy.

## 6. Prepare for the Next Day

Once morning arrives and the sun starts rising, you’re safe from the mobs. It’s time to start planning for the next phase of your survival.

### What to Do After Daybreak:

* **Gather More Resources**: Use the daylight to mine valuable materials like coal, iron, and even diamonds if you’re lucky. This will help you progress in the game.
* **Upgrade Your Shelter**: Add a door to your shelter, expand it, and craft a bed to skip the night in the future.
* **Explore Your Surroundings**: Look around for nearby resources such as villages, caves, or biomes. Exploring will open up more opportunities for survival and progression.

### Conclusion

Surviving your first night in Minecraft is a combination of preparation, quick thinking, and smart decision-making. By gathering essential resources, building a shelter, crafting basic tools, and staying safe, you can successfully make it through the first night. Once you survive, you’ll be ready to explore, mine, and thrive in the vast world of Minecraft.

Are you ready for your first night? Share your experience in the comments and let us know how you survived!

### [Minecraft 101: A Beginner’s Guide to Crafting and Surviving in Minecraft](https://aulaindia.com/minecraft-101-a-beginners-guide-to-crafting-and-surviving-in-minecraft/)

[13/01/2024](https://aulaindia.com/minecraft-101-a-beginners-guide-to-crafting-and-surviving-in-minecraft/)  [Aula India](https://aulaindia.com/category/aula-india/)

[](https://aulaindia.com/minecraft-101-a-beginners-guide-to-crafting-and-surviving-in-minecraft/)

Minecraft, a game that has captured the imagination of millions worldwide, is a realm where creativity and survival coexist. This sandbox game, developed by Mojang, offers an expansive world of possibilities. Whether you’re a new player stepping into this blocky universe for the first time or a returning player looking to refine your skills, this guide will navigate you through the essentials of crafting and surviving in the world of Minecraft.

[Gaming Keyboards](https://aulaindia.com/product-category/keyboard/) | [Gaming Mouse](https://aulaindia.com/product-category/mouse/)

## Understanding the Basics:

Before diving into crafting and survival tactics, it’s crucial to understand the core elements of Minecraft. The game features two primary modes: Survival and Creative. In Survival mode, players must gather resources, maintain health and hunger, and fend off mobs (in-game creatures). Creative mode, on the other hand, offers unlimited resources and the freedom to build without survival challenges.

## Starting Your Journey in Survival Mode:

As you spawn into a new world in Survival mode, your first objective is to gather resources. Punching trees (by left-clicking) will drop wood blocks, which are fundamental for crafting basic tools.

**Crafting 101:**

**Crafting is a quintessential aspect of Minecraft. Here’s how to get started:**

1. **Create a Crafting Table:**

* Convert wooden blocks into wooden planks by opening your inventory (default key ‘E’) and placing the wood in the crafting area.
  + Use four wooden planks to create a crafting table, providing a 3×3 crafting grid for more complex items.

* **Basic Tools:**

* Initially, create wooden tools. Craft sticks using wooden planks, then use sticks and planks to make a pickaxe, an axe, a shovel, and a sword.
  + A pickaxe is crucial for mining stone and ores, an axe for chopping wood efficiently, a shovel for digging soil or sand, and a sword for defending against mobs.

* **Upgrade Your Tools:**

* Mine stone with your wooden pickaxe and upgrade to stone tools, which are more durable and efficient.
  + Eventually, you’ll mine ores like iron and craft even stronger tools.

**Survival Essentials:**

1. **Building a Shelter:**

* Construct a basic shelter before nightfall to protect yourself from hostile mobs like zombies and skeletons. It can be as simple as a hole in the side of a hill or a small wooden structure.
  + Ensure your shelter has a door and torches (crafted from coal and sticks) to provide light and prevent mob spawning.

* **Finding Food:**

* Maintain your hunger bar to heal any lost health. Hunt animals like chickens, cows, and pigs for meat.
  + You can also farm by planting seeds from breaking grass and harvesting wheat.

* **Mining for Resources:**

* Mine for coal, iron, and other materials. Coal is used for torches and smelting, while iron is crucial for crafting stronger tools and armor.
  + Always carry torches, a sword, and food when mining, and never dig straight down.

* **Smelting:**

* Create a furnace using eight cobblestone blocks. Furnaces allow you to smelt ores into usable metal ingots and cook food.

* **Exploration and Mapping:**

* Explore the world to discover resources, biomes, and structures like villages. Craft a map using paper (from sugar cane) and a compass (iron ingots and redstone) to keep track of your location.

* **Defending Against Mobs:**

* Craft armor from leather, iron, gold, or diamond to protect against attacks.
  + Stay vigilant, especially at night, and keep your shelter secure.

## Advanced Crafting and Tips:

**As you progress, you’ll encounter more complex crafting recipes and challenges:**

1. **Enchanting:**

* Craft an enchantment table and use experience points to enchant tools, weapons, and armor with various buffs.

* **Potion Brewing:**

* Brew potions for healing, fire resistance, or underwater breathing using a brewing stand, water bottles, and various ingredients.

* **Redstone Mechanics:**

* Use redstone to create electrical circuits, enabling complex mechanisms like automatic doors and traps.

* **The Nether and The End:**

* Venture into other dimensions like The Nether and The End for advanced exploration and challenges. Prepare well, as these realms are dangerous.

[Gaming Headsets](https://aulaindia.com/product-category/headset/) | [Gaming Mouse Pads](https://aulaindia.com/product-category/mouse-pad/)

**Conclusion:**

Minecraft is a game of endless possibilities and learning curves. From the basics of crafting your first set of tools to venturing into treacherous realms, each step in Minecraft is a blend of creativity, strategy, and survival. Remember, the world of Minecraft is what you make of it. Whether you choose to build monumental structures, delve into deep cave systems, or simply enjoy the tranquility of farming, your Minecraft adventure is uniquely yours. Grab your pickaxe, let your imagination run wild, and embrace the journey of crafting and surviving in the captivating world of Minecraft.

sources.title: 10 best tips to help you survive your first night in Minecraft
sources.snippet:
# 10 best tips to help you survive your first night in Minecraft

[TOI Sports Desk](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/toireporter/author-TOI-Sports-Desk-479262519.cms) / TIMESOFINDIA.COM /  Mar 29, 2025, 18:26 IST

Share

AA

**Text Size**

* Small
* Medium
* Large

New players in Minecraft can struggle with their first night due to various mobs. Key tips for survival include gathering wood, crafting essential tools, building a quick shelter, mining stone for better tools, lighting the shelter with torches, gathering food, avoiding hostile mobs, setting a spawn point with a bed, keeping track of coordinates, and planning for the next day.



Image via Mojang

Minecraft has established itself as one of the most popular games, and the game allows you to explore a massive world, while doing loads of unique things. If you are new to the sandbox title, you could end up struggling to survive your first night in the overworld, due to the various mobs that spawn after the sun sets.   
As a result, you will need to properly prepare yourself and ensure that you end up making it through the night to progress further in the game without losing your equipment. Here are 10 essential tips to help you survive your first night in Minecraft.

## 10 Best Tips to Help You Survive Your First Night in Minecraft

Surviving Your First Night! ▫ Minecraft 1.20 Survival Guide ▫ Tutorial Let's Play [S3 Ep.1]

### 1. Gather Wood Immediately

The first suggestion for you is to gather wood immediately, as it is one of the most essential resources that you can find as you are getting started in Minecraft. The wood will enable you to make tools, a crafting table, and even a simple shelter. As this particular resource is the foundation of survival, you must ensure that you gather the logs as soon as possible.

### 2. Craft Essential Tools

Minecraft has a variety of different tools like pickaxe, sword, shovel, and more, which make the process of collecting resources and surviving a lot easier. Essentially, a pickaxe allows you to mine stone, an axe speeds up wood collection, and a sword helps you deal damage to mobs.

### 3. Build a Quick Shelter

Building a shelter will be a vital part of you surviving through the first night. There are a few different things that you can do to get shelter in Minecraft. Firstly, the fastest way would be digging into a hill or underground to create a small cave. Alternatively, you may construct a basic 4x4 or 5x5 wooden hut with a door to keep mobs out.

### 4. Mine Stone for Better Tools

After you are done building your shelter and have the necessary resources, you can further mine stone to craft stronger tools such as a stone pickaxe and sword. These particular tools will be more durable than wooden ones, and you will love using them to gather resources in the coming days.

### 5. Light Up Your Shelter with Torches

A crucial thing that you have to keep in mind is that darkness allows hostile mobs to spawn. This is why you must lighten up your shelter. For the same, you will need torches, which can be crafted using wood and coal.

### 6. Gather Food to Avoid Starvation

Food is the essence of life, and the same remains true in Minecraft. Having food is important to maintain your health and prevent hunger inside the game, and you must start hunting for food to survive. Basically, you can find animals like cows, pigs, and chickens, and cook their meat in a furnace.

### 7. Avoid Hostile Mobs

Mobs are the main danger and they are extremely tough to deal with during the first night, as you are just starting out. Thus, after the sun sets, you are advised to stay inside your shelter and avoid unnecessary fights.

### 8. Set Your Spawn Point with a Bed

Spawn Point can be set in Minecraft using a bed. Accordingly, if you find sheep before nightfall, craft a bed using three wool and three wooden planks. Another advantage of this is that sleeping in a bed skips the night.

### 9. Keep an Eye on Your Coordinates

You can press F3 (Java Edition) or open the map (Bedrock Edition) to mark the coordinates of where you are building your shelter. If you die, you can use the coordinates to find your shelter again.

### 10. Plan for the Next Day

Finally, you must also plan for the next day, as surviving the initial day won’t be enough. Once daylight returns, focus on gathering more resources, improving your shelter, and exploring. The baby steps that you can take would be to start looking for iron, expanding your food supply, and crafting better tools.   
If you have a strong start, you will be able to ensure long-term survival in Minecraft. By following these 10 tips, you can safely make it through your first night and build a good foundation.

mining-smelting.reasoning: 可用來源與重點：(1) [1] 提供熔爐 GUI 三槽位、啟動條件、每件耗時 10 秒、燃料條機制與停工條件，以及高爐/煙燻爐 5 秒且同燃料成本、各自限制；(2) [2] 提供早期採礦目標：往下到第 16 層水平找 Raw Iron、Java 可用 F3 看座標，並指出 Raw Iron 需先冶煉成 Iron Ingot 才能做鐵裝/鐵鎬；(3) [4] 提供盔甲製作成本（全套 24 單位材料）與鐵套防禦值 15。內容結構規劃：用「原料→加工→升級」類比開場→用熔爐操作流程作為本節點對『冶煉』的工作性定義（避免宣告官方定義）→解釋燃料條、停工與效率瓶頸→連回實務升級：Raw Iron→Iron Ingot→鐵裝里程碑，並點出高爐/煙燻爐是效率升級。
mining-smelting.explanation:
把早期生存想成「帶著一袋原石回到工坊」：你挖到的多半是原料（例如 raw iron），真正能把你撐到更遠探索的，是把原料加工成可用材料，再把材料變成更耐打的裝備。

在這個節點中，你可以把「冶煉」理解為一個固定流程：打開熔爐介面後，將要加工的物品放在左上輸入格、燃料放在左下燃料格，成品會累積到右側輸出格；當輸入與燃料都就位且輸出格有空間時，熔爐就會開始運作，而每處理 1 個物品需要 10 秒（200 ticks）。[1]

核心原理在「燃料條」：熔爐一開始就會立刻消耗一個燃料物品來填滿燃料量表，且燃料量表會隨時間下降，就算輸入格暫時沒有東西也可能繼續燒掉燃料；相反地，若輸出格塞滿或輸出物不相容，熔爐會暫停處理（但可能仍在燃燒）。[1] 這就是為什麼新手常覺得「我明明有燒，怎麼沒出貨」：問題往往不是配方，而是輸出塞住或燃料被空燒掉。

實務上，你的第一個里程碑通常是鐵：Game8 的路線建議往下到第 16 層再水平挖找 raw iron（Java 可按 F3 看座標），並且要先用熔爐把 raw iron 冶煉成 iron ingot，才能開始做鐵鎬與鐵裝。[2] 當你能穩定產出鐵錠後，全套盔甲需要 24 單位材料，而鐵全套提供 15 點防禦值，會讓你下礦與戰鬥的容錯大幅提升。[3] 若你開始大量處理礦物或食物，高爐/煙燻爐能以 5 秒完成 1 個物品、燃料消耗/件與一般熔爐相同；但高爐只處理礦物、煙燻爐只煮食物，其他物品仍需一般熔爐。[1]
key_points: 熔爐有三個槽位（輸入、燃料、輸出）；當輸入與燃料都放好且輸出格有空間就會開始處理，且每件物品需 10 秒。[1]
燃料會在開始時立刻被消耗並填滿燃料條，燃料條會隨時間下降，就算輸入格暫時空了也可能繼續燒掉燃料。[1]
熔爐可能因為「沒可冶煉物、沒燃料、輸出格滿/不相容、或熔爐被破壞」而停止或暫停處理；其中輸出格問題會讓處理暫停。[1]
早期裝備升級的關鍵鏈是 raw iron →（熔爐）→ iron ingot → 鐵鎬/鐵防具；一套盔甲需要 24 單位材料。[2][3]
高爐與煙燻爐處理速度是一般熔爐的 2 倍（5 秒/件），且每件消耗燃料量相同；高爐只處理礦物、煙燻爐只煮食物。[1]
examples: 情境 1：你把 raw iron 丟進熔爐卻沒看到產出，先檢查右側輸出格是不是已經有別種物品佔著（例如先前留下的其他輸出物）。輸出不相容或滿格會讓處理暫停。[1]
情境 2：你只放 1 個 raw iron 卻塞進一個燃料後就跑去探險，回來發現燃料燒掉不少但產出很少。因為燃料會先被消耗並持續燃燒，即使輸入格空了燃料條也會下降；要減少浪費就一次放多一些可冶煉物。[1]
情境 3：依照路線往下到第 16 層附近水平挖，找到 raw iron 後帶回地面用熔爐變成 iron ingot，再開始做鐵鎬與鐵裝，讓後續下礦更安全、更能長時間探索。[2][3]

sources.title: Smelting
sources.snippet:
### Furnace

[ | ]

Main article: [Furnace](/w/Furnace "Furnace")

The furnace interface contains three item slots: the upper left slot for the item that needs to be smelted, the lower left slot for fuel, and the right slot where output items accumulate and can be retrieved by the player. Flames above the fuel slot act as a gauge showing the amount of fuel left of the current fuel item. An arrow in the middle shows the progress of smelting the current item.

The furnace takes 10 seconds (200 in-game ticks) to smelt an item. It begins to smelt if both input item and fuel are placed into the corresponding slots, and there is space in the output slot.

When starting, a fuel item is consumed immediately, filling the fuel gauge. Different fuels will fuel the furnace for different amounts of time. The fuel gauge indicates how much of that fuel's burn time remains, and gradually decreases even if the input slot becomes empty. When a fuel item is fully consumed and the input slot is not empty, another one is taken from the fuel slot, and the gauge resets.

The furnace processes one input item at a time, which remains in the input slot during the 10-second process. So if multiple types of items or more than one stack of item need to be smelted, the player need to move in the item manually or using hoppers.

The arrow indicates the progress on how much the input has been smelted and how much more it needs to be smelted. When the arrow is full, the input item is removed from the input stack and an output item is added to the output stack. Smelting of the next input item then begins immediately.

Furnaces stop smelting under any of four conditions:

* When the furnace runs out of smeltable items: The input slot becomes empty or contains item that cannot be smelted.
* The furnace runs out of fuel: The fuel input slot is empty, *and* the current fuel item is fully consumed (that is, the fuel gauge becomes empty).
* The output slot becomes full: Either the slot has a full stack of the output, or the output contains the wrong output item for the current input item (for example, if the output contains [iron ingots](/w/Iron_Ingot "Iron Ingot"), but the input contains [raw beef](/w/Raw_beef "Raw beef")). In this case, smelting (but not fuel consumption) is paused until the output slot becomes available (usually because items were removed by either a player or a hopper). If a fuel item burns out in this condition, a new one is not used until the output slot is again available.
* The furnace is broken: The contents of all the slots, any accumulated experience, and the furnace itself are dropped. The currently-burning fuel item is lost since it is removed from the fuel slot before burning begins. The furnace block itself may not be dropped if it was destroyed by an explosion.

If smelting stops while a fuel item is still burning, the furnace continues to run visually, but no more input items are processed. If the fuel has been exhausted when an item has been partly smelted, the smelting progress is undone at double speed, and the item remains in the input stack.

Smelting is suspended if the [chunk](/w/Chunk "Chunk") the furnace is in becomes unloaded. It resumes when the chunk is loaded again.

### Blast furnace and smoker

[ | ]

[Smokers](/w/Smoker "Smoker") and [blast furnaces](/w/Blast_furnace "Blast furnace") use the same GUI interface as regular furnaces and function similarly to regular furnaces. They smelt twice as quickly as furnaces, requiring only 5 seconds (100 game ticks) to smelt 1 item; they consume the same amount of fuel as regular furnaces per item smelted. Blast furnaces can only smelt ores, while smokers can only cook food; any other item can be smelted only in regular furnaces.

## Recipes

[ | ]

All smelting recipes can be used in the furnace, but only subsets are available in the blast furnace and smoker.

The furnace, blast furnace and smoker keep track of experience for each item as smelting is completed for them, accumulating it in a hidden counter. The counter remembers the total earned experience even if a hopper is used to remove the items from the output slot. Experience is awarded to the player who uses the interface to remove items manually, after which the counter is reset. If the player takes some of the output but leaves some in the slot, the experience corresponding to items left in the furnace is retained and not awarded to the player. For fractional experience values, first multiply this value by the number of smelted items removed from the furnace, then award the player the integer part, and if there is a fractional part remaining, this represents the chance of an additional experience point.

* For example, when smelting 1 [coal ore](/w/Coal_ore "Coal ore") and removing the coal, the value is 0.1, so every ten [coal](/w/Coal "Coal") removed grants one experience point on average.
* When smelting 6 [sea pickles](/w/Sea_pickle "Sea pickle") and removing all 6 [lime dye](/w/Lime_dye "Lime dye"), the value is 0.2×6 = 1.2, so this grants 1 point, plus a 20% chance of another 1 point.
* The fractional experience stays within the furnace when the final total is not an integer, so the leftover experience is attributed to the next round of smelting.

### Ores (part 1)

[ | ]

All ore recipes can be used in a furnace or blast furnace.

The following additional [ores](/w/Ore "Ore") *can* be smelted, but it's more efficient to mine them with an appropriate pickaxe. In most cases mining them saves fuel and yields more product and experience, especially if the pickaxe has a [Fortune](/w/Fortune "Fortune") enchantment. Smelting them, though, allows obtaining them from an automatic device. The ore blocks themselves can be obtained only with the [Silk Touch](/w/Silk_Touch "Silk Touch") enchantment.

### Gear (part 1)

[ | ]

These recipes can be used in a furnace or blast furnace to recycle unneeded gear (tools, weapons, armor and horse armor).

## Fuel (part 1)

[ | ]

See also: [Tutorial:Smelting](/w/Tutorial:Smelting "Tutorial:Smelting")

There are multiple fuels that can be used to smelt items.

A single [lava bucket](/w/Lava_bucket "Lava bucket") or a [block of coal](/w/Block_of_coal "Block of coal") can smelt more items than can fit in the furnace, a lava bucket being able to smelt 100 blocks and a [block of coal](/w/Block_of_coal "Block of coal") being able to smelt 80 —both input and output are limited to a maximum of a [stack](/w/Stack "Stack").

## Hopper automation

[ | ]

See also: [Tutorial:Hopper § Automatic smelting](/w/Tutorial:Hopper#Automatic_smelting "Tutorial:Hopper")

[](/w/File:Automated_furnace_schematic_(with_legends).png)

In-game Automated furnace schematic

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |

[](/w/Help:Schematic "Help:Schematic")Automated furnace

The smelting process can be automated with [hoppers](/w/Hopper "Hopper") on the top and bottom of the furnace. For larger smelting jobs, a third hopper on the side of the furnace can feed in fuel and, in case of lava being used as fuel, any empty [buckets](/w/Bucket "Bucket") come out of the bottom hopper. This automatically feeds and empties the furnace so that different materials can be smelted in the same batch with no loss.

Whenever a hopper or minecart with hopper removes items from a furnace, any experience earned from cooking or smelting the removed items is saved in the furnace and awarded to the next player who either breaks the furnace or manually removes an item from the furnace's output slot. This saved experience is in addition to that earned for the manually removed item(s).

sources.title: Walkthrough and Progression Guide: How to Beat the Game
sources.snippet:
### Objectives Summary

|  | Objectives |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Make a starter base |
| 2 | Mine some **Raw Iron** |
| 3 | Get a **Diamond Pickaxe** |
| 4 | Mine for **Obsidian** |
| 5 | Dive into the **Nether** |
| 6 | Find a **Nether Fortress** |
| 7 | Farm **Ender Pearls** |
| 8 | Find a **Stronghold** |
| 9 | Enter the End Portal and defeat the **Ender Dragon** |

### Make a starter base



#### Gather some logs

The first thing that you need to do is to set up a simple base complete with the necessities for surviving. Start gathering logs by punching trees around you.

#### Use wooden planks to build a base

Use these logs to create wooden planks, which you'll be using to build your new home. Find a nice spot where resources like wood and stone are accessible, then start placing the planks.

#### Craft a Crafting Table and a Wooden Pickaxe

Next step is to create a **Crafting Table** for... crafting. This block is where all the magic begins and you'll be using it throughout your journey. Craft some **Sticks** and through the Crafting Table, craft a **Wooden Pickaxe** so you can mine stones.

#### Craft other necessities

Make sure your starter base has a **Door** and enough lighting using **Torches** to keep mobs away. Also give it a big enough room where you can place a **Furnace** (for smelting), a **Large Chest** (for storing items), and a **Bed** (for setting respawn points and skipping time).

### Mine some Raw Iron

You're done on the "craft" part, for now, so it's time to do the "mine" part. Craft a **Stone Pickaxe**, head out to the nearest cave, or create your own, then start digging down until you reach layer 16. You can check your coordinates by pressing **F3** for Java edition players or typing **/gamerule showcoordinates true** for Bedrock edition players.

Once you reach the said layer, start digging horizontally to find some **Raw Iron**. Your goal is to mine enough Raw Iron to craft a full Iron Armor and an **Iron Pickaxe**—a surprise tool that will help us later.

Take note that you need to smelt the Raw Iron using the Furnace first and turn them into **Iron Ingots** before you can use them to craft armor and tools.

[1.18 Ore Distribution](https://game8.co/games/Minecraft/archives/377343)

### Get a Diamond Pickaxe

After setting up your base, finding a steady food source, and gearing up, it's time to dig deeper into the Overworld to find **Diamonds**. Craft some more Iron Pickaxes and torches, then dig down until you reach any Y-level between-54 and -59. This is an ideal level range to setup an underground base and look for **Diamond Ores**.

Same as before with Iron, you'd want to gather enough Diamonds to craft a full set of Diamond Armor and a **Diamond Pickaxe**.

[Best Way to Mine Diamonds](https://game8.co/games/Minecraft/archives/377339)

### Mine for Obsidian

The fastest way to get Obsidian is to pour a **Water Bucket** into a Lava block then mine the resulting Obsidian block with a Diamond Pickaxe. Lava lakes are usually found on levels close to where Diamond Ores are generated.

You need at least 10 Obsidian Blocks for the next step.

sources.title: Armor - Minecraft Wiki
sources.snippet:
## Obtaining[]



### Crafting[]

A full set of armor consists of 24 units of a given material. A [turtle shell](/wiki/Turtle_shell "Turtle shell") is a [helmet](/wiki/Helmet "Helmet") with no corresponding armor pieces. Despite there being chain items, chainmail armor cannot be crafted. A full suit of Netherite armor needs 4 units of [smithing templates](/wiki/Smithing_template "Smithing template") and 4 units of Netherite ingots (16 netherite scraps and 16 gold ingots); obtaining netherite armor requires combining a diamond armor piece with a netherite ingot and smithing template using a [smithing table](/wiki/Smithing_table "Smithing table").

Armor can be [repaired](/wiki/Item_Repair "Item Repair") by placing two pieces of the same type (e.g., iron helmets) in a [grindstone](/wiki/Grindstone "Grindstone") or in the crafting grid. The resulting item has 5% more [durability](/wiki/Item_durability "Item durability") left than the original [items](/wiki/Item "Item") combined, if the total durability of both of the armor pieces is more than the total durability that a piece of armor would have fresh from a [crafting table](/wiki/Crafting_table "Crafting table"), but any [enchantments](/wiki/Enchanting "Enchanting") are lost. Repairing armor with an [anvil](/wiki/Anvil "Anvil") preserves and combines the enchantments, with a rising [experience](/wiki/Experience "Experience") cost for subsequent repairs on the same item.

| Name | Ingredients | [hide][Crafting](/wiki/Crafting "Crafting") recipe |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Helmet](/wiki/Helmet "Helmet") | [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") or [Copper Ingot](/wiki/Copper_Ingot "Copper Ingot") or [Gold Ingot](/wiki/Gold_Ingot "Gold Ingot") or [Iron Ingot](/wiki/Iron_Ingot "Iron Ingot") or [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond "Diamond") |  |
| [Chestplate](/wiki/Chestplate "Chestplate") | [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") or [Copper Ingot](/wiki/Copper_Ingot "Copper Ingot") or [Gold Ingot](/wiki/Gold_Ingot "Gold Ingot") or [Iron Ingot](/wiki/Iron_Ingot "Iron Ingot") or [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond "Diamond") |  |
| [Leggings](/wiki/Leggings "Leggings") | [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") or [Copper Ingot](/wiki/Copper_Ingot "Copper Ingot") or [Gold Ingot](/wiki/Gold_Ingot "Gold Ingot") or [Iron Ingot](/wiki/Iron_Ingot "Iron Ingot") or [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond "Diamond") |  |
| [Boots](/wiki/Boots "Boots") | [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") or [Copper Ingot](/wiki/Copper_Ingot "Copper Ingot") or [Gold Ingot](/wiki/Gold_Ingot "Gold Ingot") or [Iron Ingot](/wiki/Iron_Ingot "Iron Ingot") or [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond "Diamond") |  |
| [Turtle Shell](/wiki/Turtle_Shell "Turtle Shell") | [Turtle Scute](/wiki/Turtle_Scute "Turtle Scute") |  |

### Upgrading[]

| Ingredients | [Smithing](/wiki/Smithing_Table "Smithing Table") recipe |
| --- | --- |
| [Netherite Upgrade](/wiki/Netherite_Upgrade "Netherite Upgrade") + [Diamond Boots](/wiki/Diamond_Boots "Diamond Boots") or [Diamond Helmet](/wiki/Diamond_Helmet "Diamond Helmet") or [Diamond Leggings](/wiki/Diamond_Leggings "Diamond Leggings") or [Diamond Chestplate](/wiki/Diamond_Chestplate "Diamond Chestplate") + [Netherite Ingot](/wiki/Netherite_Ingot "Netherite Ingot") | Upgrade Gear |

### Repairing[]



#### [Unit repair](/wiki/Anvil_mechanics#Unit_repair "Anvil mechanics")[]

A piece of armor can be repaired in an anvil by adding units of the armor [material](/wiki/Material "Material")'s repair material, with each repair material restoring 25% of the armor item's maximum durability, rounded down. Chainmail armor can be repaired in an anvil using [iron ingots](/wiki/Iron_ingots "Iron ingots"). Netherite armor can be repaired in an anvil using [netherite Ingots](/wiki/Netherite_Ingots "Netherite Ingots").

## Usage[]

To have any protective effect, armor must be worn by the [player](/wiki/Player "Player"). A [helmet](/wiki/Helmet "Helmet"), [chestplate](/wiki/Chestplate "Chestplate"), [leggings](/wiki/Leggings "Leggings"), and [boots](/wiki/Boots "Boots") are equipped by placing them in the head, chest, legs and feet slots of the [inventory](/wiki/Inventory "Inventory") next to the [player](/wiki/Player "Player")'s character, respectively. Armor can also be equipped by right clicking while held. If the respective slot already contains a piece of armor, they are swapped, and the original armor ends up in the player's hand.

A chestplate provides the most protection per unit of material, followed by leggings. For leather, iron, and diamond armor, boots have equivalent armor points as the helmet, but for chainmail and gold armor, the helmet trumps boots. A [turtle shell](/wiki/Turtle_shell "Turtle shell"), in addition to providing protection, also gives the player the [Water Breathing](/wiki/Water_Breathing "Water Breathing") status effect.

Each armor piece consumes one inventory slot. They cannot be stacked.

If wearing any piece of gold armor, [Piglins](/wiki/Piglin "Piglin") become neutral towards the player, however [Piglin Brutes](/wiki/Piglin_Brute "Piglin Brute") remain hostile. If wearing any piece of leather armor, a player is immune to [freezing](/wiki/Powder_Snow "Powder Snow") damage and FOV change. If wearing leather boots, players may walk on [powder snow](/wiki/Powder_Snow "Powder Snow"). This grants the "Light as a Rabbit" [achievement](/wiki/Achievement "Achievement").

### Tiers[]

Main article: [Armor materials](/wiki/Armor_materials "Armor materials")

There are seven different types of materials of armor, including turtle shells. The six full sets are shown below in order from weakest (offers the least protection and is the least durable) to strongest (offers the most protection and is the most durable).

* [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") (on Steve)
* [Golden](/wiki/Gold_Ingot "Gold Ingot") (on Steve)
* Chainmail (on Steve)
* [Iron](/wiki/Iron_Ingot "Iron Ingot") (on Steve)
* [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond "Diamond") (on Steve)
* [Netherite](/wiki/Netherite_Ingot "Netherite Ingot") (on Steve)

* [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") (on Alex)
* [Golden](/wiki/Gold_Ingot "Gold Ingot") (on Alex)
* Chainmail (on Alex)
* [Iron](/wiki/Iron_Ingot "Iron Ingot") (on Alex)
* [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond "Diamond") (on Alex)
* [Netherite](/wiki/Netherite_Ingot "Netherite Ingot") (on Alex)

The [Turtle Shell](/wiki/Turtle_Shell "Turtle Shell") is not part of a full set. However, its defense points match gold, chainmail, and iron [helmets](/wiki/Helmet "Helmet"), while its [durability](/wiki/Item_durability "Item durability") is between iron and diamond helmets (276). In the game's code, its protection is equivalent to iron armor.

### Smelting ingredient[]

| Name | Ingredients | [hide][Smelting](/wiki/Smelting "Smelting") recipe |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Iron Nugget](/wiki/Iron_Nugget "Iron Nugget") | Any [iron armor](/wiki/Iron_armor "Iron armor") or Any [chainmail armor](/wiki/Chainmail_armor "Chainmail armor") + Any [fuel](/wiki/Fuel "Fuel") | 0.1 |
| [Gold Nugget](/wiki/Gold_Nugget "Gold Nugget") | Any [golden armor](/wiki/Golden_armor "Golden armor") + Any [fuel](/wiki/Fuel "Fuel") | 0.1 |

### Defense points[]

The armor bar as shown in game

Armor defense points are controlled by an [attribute](/wiki/Attribute "Attribute"), `generic.armor`. The [player](/wiki/Player "Player")'s current protection level is represented visually by the armor bar. The armor meter is affected by the particular pieces that are worn as well as the tier of the armor. The following table shows the number of defense points added by default by each individual piece of armor, as well as the total points added by a full set of armor for each material.

| Material | Full set | Helmet / Cap | Chestplate / Tunic | Leggings / Pants | Boots |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Turtle Shell | 2 () | 2 () | N/A | N/A | N/A |
| Leather | 7 () | 1 () | 3 () | 2 () | 1 () |
| Golden | 11 ( × 5.5) | 2 () | 5 () | 3 () | 1 () |
| Chainmail | 12 ( × 6) | 2 () | 5 () | 4 () | 1 () |
| Iron | 15 ( × 7.5) | 2 () | 6 () | 5 () | 2 () |
| Diamond | 20 ( × 10) | 3 () | 8 () | 6 () | 3 () |
| Netherite | 20 ( × 10) | 3 () | 8 () | 6 () | 3 () |

Different combinations of armor provide different levels of defense.

### Enchantments[]

See also: [Enchanting](/wiki/Enchanting "Enchanting")

Armor can be [enchanted](/wiki/Enchanting "Enchanting") to provide various enchantments. Enchantments can provide more protection or allow armor to protect certain types of damage that armor doesn't normally protect against, such as fall [damage](/wiki/Damage "Damage") or [fire](/wiki/Fire "Fire"). Damage reduction from enchantments does not decrease the armor's [durability](/wiki/Item_durability "Item durability"). Armor enchantments do not appear on the armor bar.

An armor's material determines how enchantable it is. The higher a material's enchantability, the greater the chances of getting multiple and high-level enchantments (see [enchantment mechanics](/wiki/Enchantment_mechanics "Enchantment mechanics") for details).

| [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") | [Golden](/wiki/Gold "Gold") | Chainmail | [Iron](/wiki/Iron "Iron") | [Turtle Shell](/wiki/Turtle_Shell "Turtle Shell") | [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond "Diamond") | [Netherite](/wiki/Netherite_Ingot "Netherite Ingot") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 15 | 25 | 12 | 9 | 9 | 10 | 15 |

As with several [enchantments](/wiki/Enchanting "Enchanting"), several different levels of protection are possible. The maximum level of a protection enchantment is currently IV (4). Protection enchantments from multiple pieces of armor stack together, up to a calculated maximum.

Each protection enchantment protects against specific types of [damage](/wiki/Damage "Damage"). The amount of damage reduction depends on the **Enchantment Protection Factor** (EPF) provided by that enchantment.

| Enchantment | Damage reduced for | Type Modifier | EPF Level I | EPF Level II | EPF Level III | EPF Level IV |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Protection | All | 1 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 |
| Fire Protection | [Fire](/wiki/Fire "Fire"), [lava](/wiki/Lava "Lava"), and [blaze](/wiki/Blaze "Blaze") fireballs | 2 | 2 | 4 | 6 | 8 |
| Blast Protection | [Explosions](/wiki/Explosion "Explosion") | 2 | 2 | 4 | 6 | 8 |
| Projectile Protection | [Arrows](/wiki/Bow "Bow"), thrown [tridents](/wiki/Tridents "Tridents"), [ghast](/wiki/Ghast "Ghast") and [blaze](/wiki/Blaze "Blaze") fireballs | 2 | 2 | 4 | 6 | 8 |
| Feather Falling | Fall damage (including [ender pearls](/wiki/Ender_pearl "Ender pearl")) | 3 | 3 | 6 | 9 | 12 |

When a [player](/wiki/Player "Player") or [mob](/wiki/Mob "Mob") wearing armor is subjected to [damage](/wiki/Damage "Damage"), the EPFs of all applicable enchantments are added together, capped at 20, and then damage reduction is `cappedEPF / 25`, giving a maximum reduction of 20⁄25 (80%) at EPF 20.

Because of the caps in the calculation, it is possible to achieve maximum protection against specific types of damage with just three pieces of armor. For example, two pieces of armor with [Blast Protection](/wiki/Blast_Protection "Blast Protection") IV (EPF 8 each) and a single piece with [Protection](/wiki/Protection "Protection") IV (EPF 4) would give a total EPF of 20 versus explosions. Any additional EPF would be wasted against explosions (but might be useful against other types of damage, if applicable).

If the [damage](/wiki/Damage "Damage") is of a type that armor protects against normally, this reduction applies only to the damage that got through the armor.

It is possible using `/give` to obtain armor with an [enchantment](/wiki/Enchanting "Enchanting") level higher than what is normally obtainable via normal [survival](/wiki/Survival "Survival"). Using this method, a [player](/wiki/Player "Player") could give themselves, for example, a full set of diamond armor with a [Protection](/wiki/Protection "Protection") V enchantment on every piece. Following the algorithm above, we find that, because Protection V has an EPF of 5, the armor reaches the maximum EPF of 20 for all types of damage. Any higher Protection enchantments could be used to reach the cap with just one level XX (20) enchantment, rather than having a full set of enchanted armor, but would be wasted if all pieces shared the same level enchantment.

The table below shows all armor enchantments at their max levels:

Armor Enchantments at Max Levels

| Armor Enchantment | Max Level |
| [Protection](/wiki/Protection "Protection") | IV |
| [Fire Protection](/wiki/Fire_Protection "Fire Protection") |
| [Blast Protection](/wiki/Blast_Protection "Blast Protection") |
| [Projectile Protection](/wiki/Projectile_Protection "Projectile Protection") |
| [Feather Falling](/wiki/Feather_Falling "Feather Falling") |
| [Respiration](/wiki/Respiration "Respiration") | III |
| [Thorns](/wiki/Thorns "Thorns") |
| [Depth Strider](/wiki/Depth_Strider "Depth Strider") |
| [Unbreaking](/wiki/Unbreaking "Unbreaking") |
| [Soul Speed](/wiki/Soul_Speed "Soul Speed") |
| [Swift Sneak](/wiki/Swift_Sneak "Swift Sneak") |
| [Frost Walker](/wiki/Frost_Walker "Frost Walker") | II |
| [Aqua Affinity](/wiki/Aqua_Affinity "Aqua Affinity") | I |
| [Mending](/wiki/Mending "Mending") |
| [Curse of Binding](/wiki/Curse_of_Binding "Curse of Binding") |
| [Curse of Vanishing](/wiki/Curse_of_Vanishing "Curse of Vanishing") |

Note: Only one form of protection can be applied to a piece of armor (i.e. a chestplate cannot have both Fire Protection and Blast Protection, unless using [commands](/wiki/Command "Command") to get all protection enchants in one piece of armor). Feather Falling, Frost Walker, Depth Strider and Soul Speed are enchantments applicable only to boots. Respiration and Aqua Affinity are enchantments applicable only to helmets. Swift Sneak is enchantment applicable only to leggings.

nautilus-1211.reasoning:
可用來源與重點：
- [1][2]（官方更新說明）：
  - Nautilus 為新的中立海洋生物、生成於所有海洋生態域、陸地會窒息傷害、被挑釁會用 Dash 攻擊，且偶爾會 Dash 攻擊附近河豚。[1][2]
  - 可用河豚或河豚桶進行馴服與繁殖；與玩家互動後會變成常駐（不再自然消失）。[1][2]
  - 馴服後可裝鞍騎乘、可開啟管理鞍與盔甲的物品欄介面、按跳躍鍵觸發類似駱駝的 Dash；騎乘者獲得「Breath of the Nautilus」效果（暫停氧氣消耗但不回復）；裝鞍/不裝鞍的遊走限制分別為 16/32 格。[1][2]
  - Nautilus Armor 可裝在 Nautilus 與 Zombie Nautilus；有銅/金/鐵/鑽石/獄髓版本；部分版本可在埋藏的寶藏、海底廢墟、沉船的箱子取得；獄髓版本由鑽石版本在鍛造台升級而來。[1][2]
內容規劃：
1) 用「潛水裝備」類比建立直覺：Nautilus = 移動載具＋氧氣管理。
2) 定義：它是可馴服的中立海洋坐騎與其關鍵能力（鞍、Dash、呼吸效果、遊走限制）。
3) 原理：為何一定要先馴服再談控制/裝備；為何 Breath 是“暫停”而非“回滿”；為何有 16/32 格遊走限制。
4) 實務重要性：海洋探索與作戰風險控制；盔甲取得與最終獄髓升級路線。
nautilus-1211.explanation:
把 Nautilus 想成「你在海裡的機車＋小型氧氣系統」：它先解決移動效率，再用一個特定機制把溺水風險變得可控，讓你能更安心地把注意力放在探索與戰鬥節奏上。

定義上，Nautilus 是 1.21.11 新增的中立海洋生物，會生成在所有海洋生態域；它在陸地會受到窒息傷害，被挑釁時會用 Dash 攻擊，且偶爾也會以 Dash 攻擊附近的河豚。[1][2] 你可以用「河豚或河豚桶」來馴服與繁殖；一旦你與它互動，它就會變成常駐，不再自然消失，適合當作長期坐騎培養。[1][2]

核心運作原理是：先用餵食把它從「野生」轉成「可管理的夥伴」，接著用裝備把能力解鎖。馴服後的 Nautilus 能裝鞍騎乘，並提供管理鞍與盔甲的物品欄介面；同時具備類似駱駝的 Dash（按跳躍鍵觸發）。[1][2] 更關鍵的是，當你騎乘時會獲得「Breath of the Nautilus」，它不是幫你把氧氣補滿，而是“暫停氧氣消耗”，因此你要把它理解成「把氧氣條按下暫停鍵」而不是「回復鍵」。[1][2]

在實務上，這套機制讓海洋行動有了“停靠點”概念：你可以先在水下騎乘來鎖住氧氣消耗，必要時再下坐做短時間操作，然後回到坐騎上再度穩定局勢。[1][2] 另外，裝鞍會把牠的遊走限制縮到 16 格（不裝鞍為 32 格），更利於你在常用海域把坐騎留在可預期的位置。[1][2] 進一步的生存力則來自 Nautilus Armor：它可裝在 Nautilus／Zombie Nautilus 上，分為銅、金、鐵、鑽石、獄髓版本；其中銅/金/鐵/鑽石有機會在埋藏的寶藏、海底廢墟、沉船箱子取得，而獄髓版本需由鑽石版本在鍛造台升級而來。[1][2]
key_points: Nautilus 是 1.21.11 新增的中立海洋生物，生成於所有海洋生態域，且在陸地會受到窒息傷害。[1][2]
馴服與繁殖都使用河豚或河豚桶；與玩家互動後會變成常駐，不再自然消失，適合長期培養坐騎。[1][2]
馴服後可裝鞍騎乘並開啟坐騎物品欄管理鞍與盔甲，且能用跳躍鍵觸發類似駱駝的 Dash。[1][2]
騎乘時會獲得「Breath of the Nautilus」，效果是暫停氧氣消耗但不會回復氧氣，因此它是“凍結氧氣條”而非“補氧”。[1][2]
裝鞍/不裝鞍會影響 Nautilus 的遊走限制（16 格/32 格）；盔甲分級為銅、金、鐵、鑽石、獄髓，且獄髓由鑽石在鍛造台升級而來。[1][2]

sources.title: Minecraft: Java Edition 1.21.11 - Mounts of Mayhem
sources.snippet:
# Minecraft: Java Edition 1.21.11 - Mounts of Mayhem

Today Mounts of Mayhem charges into Minecraft, bringing a new weapon to wield, mounts to tame, foes to face, and strategies to master! Challengers await beneath scorching suns, night skies, and deep in the ocean’s depths – and each clash will demand a fresh strategy. Fight back with the spear, a new tiered weapon which deals damage based on your precision and the speed of your trusted steed. So, riders of the Overworld – mount up and lead the charge in the Mounts of Mayhem drop!

## New Features

* Added the Nautilus and the Zombie Nautilus mobs
* Added Nautilus Armor
* Added the Camel Husk mob
* Added the Parched mob
* Added Spear weapons
* Zombie Horses now spawn naturally
* Added new graphical options "Texture Filtering" and "Anisotropic Filtering"
* Added Netherite Horse Armor that can be obtained by upgrading Diamond Horse Armor using a Smithing Table

### Nautilus

* The Nautilus is a new neutral aquatic mob
* Spawns in all ocean biomes
* Attacks using a Dash attack, if provoked
* Occasionally attacks Pufferfish within range using the Dash attack
* Takes suffocation damage on land
* The Nautilus can be bred and tamed using a Pufferfish or a Bucket of Pufferfish
* Despawns naturally, however becomes persistent when interacted with by a player
* Has a chance to drop a Nautilus Shell when killed, with increasing chance for each level of Looting
* A tamed Nautilus:
  + Can be equipped with a Saddle to ride
  + Has an inventory screen for managing Saddle and Armor
  + Has a Dash skill similar to Camels, used by pressing the Jump button
  + Adds the "Breath of the Nautilus" effect to mounted players
  + With a Saddle has a roaming restriction of 16 blocks
  + Without a Saddle has a roaming restriction of 32 blocks

#### Zombie Nautilus

* The Zombie Nautilus is a new underwater mob that spawns with a Drowned rider wielding a Trident
* The Zombie Nautilus is only hostile if ridden by a hostile mob
* The Zombie Nautilus behaves similar to the Nautilus except that it cannot be bred
* Drops 0-3 Rotten Flesh when killed, with 0-1 extra for each level of Looting

##### Coral Zombie Nautilus

* The Zombie Nautilus has a Coral Zombie Nautilus variant that spawns in the Warm Ocean biome

#### Nautilus Armor

* Equippable on the Nautilus and Zombie Nautilus
* Comes in Copper, Golden, Iron, Diamond and Netherite versions
* Copper, Iron, Golden, and Diamond Nautilus Armor now have a chance to be found in the Chests for the following Structures:
  + Buried Treasure
  + Ocean Ruins
  + Shipwreck
* Netherite Nautilus Armor is created by upgrading from Diamond Nautilus Armor using a Smithing Table

### Status Effects

* Added Breath of the Nautilus effect
  + Pauses player oxygen consumption (but does not replenish oxygen)

### Spear Weapon

* New weapon that can be crafted in Wood, Stone, Copper, Iron, Gold and Diamond versions
* Also exists in a Netherite variant that is created by upgrading a Diamond Spear using a Smithing Table
* Each material has different stats for timing, influencing how fast they are to use
* Has a minimum reach for damage, being too close to a target will yield no damage
* Has extended maximum reach compared to other tools and weapons
* Bounces back visually on hit
* Has two attacks: Jab and Charge
* Does not cause item interact vibrations
* Zombies, Husks, Zombified Piglins and Piglins can spawn with the Spear
* Golden Spear is considered a preferred weapon by Piglins
* Spears can be enchanted with the same Enchantments that can be used with Swords except Sweeping Edge
* Spears can also be enchanted with the new Lunge Enchantment
* Added various Spears to various loot chests around the world
  + Iron Spears can be found in Buried Treasure chests
  + Copper and Iron Spears can be found in Weaponsmith chests in villages
  + Stone Spears can be found in Ocean Ruin chests
  + Diamond Spears can be found in Bastion Remnant and End City chests

#### Jab Attack

* Quick-press the primary action button to use
* A low damage attack with knockback
* Has a cooldown between attacks dependent on material
  + Wooden Spears have the fastest cooldown; Netherite the slowest
* Can hit multiple enemies
* Does not destroy blocks

#### Charge Attack

* Press and hold the secondary action button to use
* Damage is based on Spear material, the player’s view angle, and the velocity of both the player and the target
* While holding down the button, the attack goes through three stages before returning to idle:
  + Engaged: The Spear can deal damage, knockback, and dismount mounted enemies if the speed is above the required thresholds
  + Tired: Indicated by the Spear rotating to a vertical position and shaking
    - The Spear can deal damage and knockback (but not dismount) if the speed is above the required thresholds
  + Disengaged: Indicated by the Spear being lowered, pointing downwards
    - The Spear deals damage but not knockback or dismount if the speed is above the required threshold
* The Spear's range when used by mobs is half of that when used by players, but they can deal damage, dismount and knockback at lower speeds than the players
* Zombies, Husks, Zombified Piglins and Piglins know how to charge with a Spear

#### Lunge Enchantment

* Spear exclusive enchantment
* Only works with the Jab attack
* When doing a Jab attack with Lunge, it propels the player horizontally in the view direction
* The view angle needs to be perfectly leveled horizontally to achieve maximum lunge distance
* Using Lunge has a durability cost of 1 for using Lunge across all Enchantment levels
* Using Lunge consumes hunger points by exhausting the player, similar to sprinting or jumping
  + Level 1 Lunge consumes 1 hunger point, level 2 consumes 2 hunger points, and level 3 consumes 3 hunger points
  + Lunge can only be used if the player has at least 6 hunger points in their hunger bar
* Does not work when the player is in water or flying with Elytra

### Advancements

* Added the "Mob Kabob" advancement for spearing 5 enemies with a Charge attack

## Changes

* Horses, Mules, Donkeys, and Camels no longer sink in water while ridden by a player
  + Skeleton Horses are an exception - they still sink
* Bats no longer spawn more frequently and in higher light levels between October 20 and November 3
* Items will no longer "bob" whenever their durability changes, such as when losing durability from an attack, or repaired by Mending
* Horses, Mules, Donkeys, Camels, Zombie Horses and Camel Husks will not panic if controlled by Mobs
* Parrots can now mimic the sounds of Zombie Nautilus, Zombie Horse, Camel Husk and Parched
* Spawn Eggs have been reorganized into groups in the Creative Inventory
* Chunks now fade in instead of appearing out of nowhere, with the fade-in time adjustable through Video Settings
* Changes to visuals and visual options
* Updated the Main Menu background panorama
* Added splash text "One does not simply walk to the Far Lands"

> **Developer’s Note:** *This statement was proven false on October 4, 2025.*

### Zombie Horse

* Zombie Horses now spawn naturally
* Zombie Horses spawn in darkness, and burn in sunlight like other undead mobs
* Zombie Horses count towards the hostile mob cap and spawn with other hostile mobs
* Zombie Horses do not display any hostile behavior towards players. Their hostility resides solely in their Zombie rider
* Zombie Horses can be leashed when its mob jockey is removed
* Zombie Horses despawn like other hostile mobs
  + However, they become persistent when interacted with by a player
* Zombie Horses can be tamed in the same fashion as regular Horses, and can then be mounted and controlled by a player
* Zombie Horses can be healed and tempted using Red Mushrooms
* Tamed Zombie Horses can be equipped with a Saddle and Horse Armor
* Horse Armor protects Zombie Horses from sunlight damage
* Zombie Horses have 25 health points (12.5 hearts) of health
* Zombie Horses have variable movement speed and jump strength, determined when they spawn
* Zombie Horses drops 2-3 Rotten Flesh when killed, with extra 0-1 for each level of Looting
* Zombies riding Zombie Horses now have a chance of dropping a Red Mushroom on death

### Mob Spawning

* Zombie Horses now have a chance to spawn in plains and savanna biome types, with a zombie rider holding an iron spear

### Advancements

* Updated the "How Did We Get Here?" advancement to include the Breath of the Nautilus effect
* Updated the "Best Friends Forever" advancement to include the Nautilus, Zombie Nautilus, and the Zombie Horse
* Updated the "The Parrots and the Bats" advancement to include the Nautilus
* Updated the "Two by Two" advancement to include the Nautilus
* Updated the "Monster Hunter" advancement to include the Zombie Nautilus, the Zombie Horse, the Camel Husk and the Parched
* Updated the "A Throwaway Joke", "Take Aim" and "It Spreads" advancements to include the Nautilus, the Zombie Nautilus, the Zombie Horse, the Camel Husk and the Parched
* Updated the "Oh Shiny" advancement to include the Golden Spear and Golden Nautilus Armor

### Items

* Spawn Eggs are now sorted thematically in the Creative Inventory, according to the following categories:
  + Overworld
    - Farm Animals
    - Mounts
    - Pets
    - Wild Animals
    - Aquatic
    - Miscellaneous
    - Golems
    - Villagers
    - Undead
    - Spiders
    - Monsters
    - Illagers
  + Nether
  + The End

### Zombie Nautilus Variants

* Zombie Nautilus variants can be data-driven by adding entries to data//zombie\_nautilus\_variant/.json
* This feature is experimental
* Fields in file:
  + model - one of: normal, warm
  + asset\_id - namespaced id for this variant asset, resolves to assets//textures/.png
  + spawn\_conditions - a variant selection rule

#### Added minecraft:zombie\_nautilus/variant

* Controls the variant of a Zombie Nautilus mob
* Format: namespaced id of an entry in the zombie\_nautilus\_variant registry

#### Added minecraft:damage\_type

* Specifies the type of damage this item deals
* Format: namespaced ID of the damage type to use
  + e.g. damage\_type='minecraft:spear'

#### Added minecraft:attack\_range

* Enables a custom attack range when using the item.
* Overrides the normal entity interaction range for the player.
* Regular melee attack range is computed for Mobs based on the distance of the target hitbox to their hitbox. This component modifies that distance check by adjusting minimum and maximum padding for the attack hitbox.
* Mobs using minecraft:kinetic\_weapon will have their attack range applied in the same way it applies to players
* Format: object with fields
  + min\_reach: float, the minimum distance in blocks from the attacker to the target to be considered valid
    - Default value: 0.0, valid from 0.0 to 64.0
  + max\_reach: float, the maximum distance in blocks from the attacker to the target to be considered valid
    - Default value: 3.0, valid from 0.0 to 64.0
  + min\_creative\_reach: float, the minimum distance in blocks from the Creative Mode attacker to the target to be considered valid
    - Default value: 0.0, valid from 0.0 to 64.0
  + max\_creative\_reach: float, the maximum distance in blocks from the Creative Mode attacker to the target to be considered valid
    - Default value: 5.0, valid from 0.0 to 64.0
  + hitbox\_margin: float, the margin applied to the target bounding box when checking for valid hitbox collision
    - Default value: 0.3, valid from 0.0 to 1.0
  + mob\_factor: float, the multiplier applied to the min\_range and max\_range when checking for valid distance when the item is used by a mob
    - Default value: 1.0, valid from 0.0 to 2.0

#### Added minecraft:kinetic\_weapon

* Enables a charge-type attack when using the item (primarily for Spears), where, while being used, the damage is dealt along a ray every time an entity comes in contact with it, with the damage based on the relative speed of the entities
* Format: object with fields
  + contact\_cooldown\_ticks: integer, the cooldown in ticks after hitting, and losing contact with an entity before being able to hit it again
    - Default value: 10
  + delay\_ticks: integer, the time in ticks required before the weapon is effective
    - Default value: 0
  + dismount\_conditions, knockback\_conditions, damage\_conditions: indicating the condition to apply dismount, knockback and damage effects respectively. Objects with fields:
    - max\_duration\_ticks: integer, the ticks after which the condition is no longer checked. This starts after delay has elapsed
    - min\_speed: float, the minimum speed of the attacker, in blocks per second, along the direction that the attacker is looking
      * Optional, default value: 0.0
    - min\_relative\_speed: float, the minimum relative speed between the attacker and target, in blocks per second, along the direction that the attacker is looking
      * Optional, default value: 0.0
  + forward\_movement: float, the distance the item moves out of hand during animation
    - Default value: 0.0
  + damage\_multiplier: float, the multiplier for the final damage from the relative speed
    - Default value: 1.0
  + sound: Optional Sound Event to play when the weapon is engaged
  + hit\_sound: Optional Sound Event to play when the weapon hits an entity
  + e.g. kinetic\_weapon={forward\_movement:1.0,delay:20,damage\_conditions:{max\_duration:60},knockback\_conditions:{max\_duration:40},dismount\_conditions:{max\_duration:20}}
* The damage dealt is calculated as floor(relative\_speed \* velocity\_multiplier) where relative\_speed is the difference of speed vectors of the attacker and the target as projected onto the axis of the attacker's view vector
  + Any additional damage from enchantments or attribute modifiers is added after this calculation

#### Added minecraft:piercing\_weapon

* Enables a quick attack that damages multiple entities along a ray (primarily for Spears)
* Format: object with fields
  + deals\_knockback: boolean, whether the attack deals knockback
    - Default value: true
  + dismounts: boolean, whether the attack dismounts the target
    - Default value: false
  + sound: Optional Sound Event to play when a player attacks with the weapon
  + hit\_sound: Optional Sound Event to play when the weapon hits an entity
  + e.g. piercing\_weapon={dismounts:true}

### Advancements

* Added new spear\_mobs trigger with fields:
  + player: optional entity predicate, the player using the kinetic weapon
  + count: optional integer, the number of mobs hit in a single use of the kinetic weapon

### Damage Types

* Added a new minecraft:spear damage type

### Enchantments



#### Enchantment Effect Components

* minecraft:post\_piercing\_attack: Effects applying after a piercing attack with an item
  + Only triggers if the player has at least 6 hunger points in their hunger bar

#### Item Tags

* Added #camel\_husk\_food - all items that can be used to feed a Camel Husk
* Added #nautilus\_bucket\_food - bucketed fish items that can be used to feed a tamed Nautilus and Zombie Nautilus
* Added #nautilus\_food - all items that can be used to feed a tamed Nautilus and Zombie Nautilus
* Added #nautilus\_taming\_items - items that can be used to tame a Nautilus and a Zombie Nautilus
* Added #spears - all Spear weapons
* Added #enchantable/lunge - all items that can be enchanted with the lunge enchantment
* Added #enchantable/melee\_weapon - all primary melee weapons including Swords and Spears
* Renamed #enchantable/sword to #enchantable/sweeping

#### Biome Tags

* Added #spawns\_coral\_variant\_zombie\_nautilus - biomes where the Coral Variant of the Zombie Nautilus can spawn
* Removed #snow\_golem\_melts and #increased\_fire\_burnout - replaced by gameplay/snow\_golem\_melts and gameplay/increased\_fire\_burnout Environment Attributes
* Removed #plays\_underwater\_music - replaced by only\_underwater field in the audio/background\_music Environment Attribute
* Removed #has\_closer\_water\_fog - replaced by visual/water\_fog\_end\_distance Environment Attribute
* Removed #without\_patrol\_spawns - replaced by gameplay/can\_pillager\_patrol\_spawn Environment Attribute

#### Entity Tags

* Added #burn\_in\_daylight - entities that burn in daylight
* Added #can\_float\_while\_ridden - entities that can float on water while being ridden
* Added #can\_wear\_nautilus\_armor - entities that can equip Nautilus Armor
* Added #nautilus\_hostiles - entities that the Nautilus will be hostile towards when untamed

### Item Sprites

* Added new item sprites:
  + item/camel\_husk\_spawn\_egg
  + item/copper\_nautilus\_armor
  + item/diamond\_nautilus\_armor
  + item/golden\_nautilus\_armor
  + item/iron\_nautilus\_armor
  + item/leather\_horse\_armor\_overlay
  + item/nautilus\_spawn\_egg
  + item/netherite\_horse\_armor
  + item/netherite\_nautilus\_armor
  + item/parched\_spawn\_egg
  + item/zombie\_nautilus\_spawn\_egg
* Added the following item sprites for the Spear weapon:
  + When in inventory: item/wooden\_spear, item/stone\_spear, item/copper\_spear, item/iron\_spear, item/golden\_spear, item/diamond\_spear, item/netherite\_spear
  + When in hand: item/wooden\_spear\_in\_hand, item/stone\_spear\_in\_hand, item/copper\_spear\_in\_hand, item/iron\_spear\_in\_hand, item/golden\_spear\_in\_hand, item/diamond\_spear\_in\_hand, item/netherite\_spear\_in\_hand

### UI Sprites

* Added new UI sprites:
  + container/slot/nautilus\_armor
  + container/slot/spear - Spear icon used in the Smithing Table screen
  + container/slot/nautilus\_armor\_inventory - Nautilus icon used in Nautilus inventory UI
* Added new nine-sliced sprites:
  + container/inventory/effect\_background
  + container/inventory/effect\_background\_ambient
* Removed the following sprites:
  + container/inventory/effect\_background\_large
  + container/inventory/effect\_background\_small

### Mob Effect Sprites

* Added new mob effect sprite:
  + breath\_of\_the\_nautilus

### Entity Textures

* Added new entity textures:
  + entity/camel/camel\_husk.png
  + entity/equipment/camel\_husk\_saddle/saddle.png
  + entity/equipment/horse\_body/leather\_overlay.png
  + entity/equipment/horse\_body/netherite.png
  + entity/equipment/nautilus\_body/copper.png
  + entity/equipment/nautilus\_body/diamond.png
  + entity/equipment/nautilus\_body/gold.png
  + entity/equipment/nautilus\_body/iron.png
  + entity/equipment/nautilus\_body/netherite.png
  + entity/equipment/nautilus\_saddle/saddle.png
  + entity/equipment/zombie\_nautilus\_coral.png
  + entity/nautilus/nautilus.png
  + entity/nautilus/nautilus\_baby.png
  + entity/nautilus/zombie\_nautilus.png
  + entity/skeleton/parched.png
  + entity/skeleton/parched\_overlay.png

### Sounds

* Added new sounds for Spears:
  + item.spear.hit
  + item.spear.use
  + item.spear.attack
* Added special sounds for the Wooden Spear:
  + item.spear\_wood.hit
  + item.spear\_wood.use
  + item.spear\_wood.attack
* Added new sound events for the Lunge Enchantment:
  + item.spear.lunge\_1
  + item.spear.lunge\_2
  + item.spear.lunge\_3
* Added new sound events for the Nautilus:
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.ambient\_land
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.ambient
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.death\_land
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.death
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.eat
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.hurt\_land
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.hurt
  + entity.baby\_nautilus.swim
  + entity.nautilus.ambient\_land
  + entity.nautilus.ambient
  + entity.nautilus.dash\_land
  + entity.nautilus.dash\_ready\_land
  + entity.nautilus.dash\_ready
  + entity.nautilus.dash
  + entity.nautilus.death\_land
  + entity.nautilus.death
  + entity.nautilus.eat
  + entity.nautilus.hurt\_land
  + entity.nautilus.hurt
  + entity.nautilus.riding
  + entity.nautilus.swim
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.ambient\_land
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.ambient
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.dash\_land
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.dash\_ready\_land
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.dash\_ready
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.dash
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.death\_land
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.death
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.eat
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.hurt\_land
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.hurt
  + entity.zombie\_nautilus.swim
  + item.nautilus\_armor.equip
  + item.nautilus\_armor.unequip
  + item.nautilus\_saddle\_equip
  + item.nautilus\_saddle\_underwater\_equip
* Added new sound events for the Zombie Horse:
  + entity.zombie\_horse.angry
  + entity.zombie\_horse.eat
* Added new sound events for the Parched:
  + entity.parched.ambient
  + entity.parched.death
  + entity.parched.hurt
  + entity.parched.step
* Added new sound events for the Camel Husk:
  + entity.camel\_husk.ambient
  + entity.camel\_husk.dash
  + entity.camel\_husk.dash\_ready
